methodsAttempted.query: site:cardiff.ac.uk Cardiff University generative AI policy students assessment academic integrity ChatGPT
methodsAttempted.notes: Firecrawl search returned mostly ORCA research repository items plus public Cardiff library and teaching-academy pages.
Firecrawl search located official Cardiff public library guidance, a Cardiff Learning and Teaching Academy post, and Cardiff official academic-regulations documents.

Firecrawl search located the official Academic Regulations and Policies page and Academic Integrity Policy PDF; ORCA repository research papers were rejected as research outputs rather than institutional policy evidence.
Public Cardiff pages linked to intranet student and staff AI guidance; both resolved to Microsoft sign-in and were marked inaccessible.
Direct curl to www.cardiff.ac.uk robots and PDF URLs was challenged by Cloudflare, but Firecrawl retrieved the selected public Cardiff pages and PDFs without login bypass.
robots.txt allows public site paths outside wp-admin; the selected Information Literacy Resource Bank page was public.
robots.txt allows public blog paths for search use and disallows several named AI crawlers; this staged bundle stores only short evidence snippets and metadata.

artifacts.summary: Official Cardiff public sources support narrow claims about Cardiff's academic-regulations language on technologies such as Generative Artificial Intelligence, Cardiff library guidance to check module/course and University guidance before using AI in assessed work, the library's scoped treatment of Microsoft Copilot for literature-search topic development, and a Learning and Teaching Academy post describing intranet AI education guidance and Academic Integrity module content. Intranet AI guidance pages were treated as login-gated and not used as evidence.

artifacts.claimText: Cardiff University's 2025/26 Academic Regulations and Policies include a teaching, learning and assessment expectation that providers establish communicated approaches to technologies such as Generative Artificial Intelligence and define misuse of those technologies.

artifacts.sourceTitle: AI in Education at Cardiff University
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: Working with colleagues in the AI in Education Group, we have published updated staff guidance on the use of AI in education, now available on the intranet. This resource offers practical advice, examples, and considerations for integrating AI into teaching and learning in a responsible and pedagogically sound way. We have also worked with students to create new guidance to support them in the ethical and responsible use of generative AI technologies as part of their learning experience.

artifacts.sourceTitle: AI in Education at Cardiff University
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: In the meantime, please encourage students to complete the Academic Integrity module in their Learning Central Organisations. The module includes content on using generative AI in the context of academic integrity. By completing the module, students develop their understanding of honest, ethical and responsible conduct when submitting academic work.

artifacts.notes: Approved as a narrow claim about Cardiff's 2025/26 regulations reproducing a teaching, learning and assessment expectation for technologies such as Generative Artificial Intelligence; not promoted as a canonical public claim.
Approved because the public Cardiff library source directly states this instruction; the linked intranet student AI guidance remains inaccessible and was not used as evidence.
Approved only for the literature-searching context; this is not a blanket institutional approval of Copilot for all assessment work.
Approved as a blog-reported description of Cardiff AI education guidance activity; not treated as direct access to the intranet guidance itself.
Approved because the public LTA post directly says the Academic Integrity module includes generative AI content in the context of academic integrity.
Approved as general academic-integrity context only; the Academic Integrity Policy PDF excerpt used here does not itself mention generative AI.

artifacts.title: Cardiff University AI Policy Staged Source Review
artifacts.summary: Cardiff University's public evidence set includes 2025/26 academic-regulations language on technologies such as Generative Artificial Intelligence, public library guidance to check course/module and University guidance before using AI for assessed work, a scoped library statement about Microsoft Copilot for literature-search topic development, and a Learning and Teaching Academy post describing intranet AI education guidance and Academic Integrity module content. Student and staff intranet AI guidance pages were login-gated and not used as direct evidence.
limitations: This tracker is not legal advice, not academic integrity advice, and not an official university statement unless a linked source is the university's own official page.
The student and staff AI education guidance pages linked from public Cardiff pages were intranet login-gated and were not used as direct evidence.
The Cardiff Learning and Teaching Academy source is a public blog post; related claims are scoped as blog-reported guidance activity, not binding institutional policy.
The Academic Integrity Policy claim is general academic-integrity context and is not AI-specific.
artifacts.sourceRightsPolicy: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.
